source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A $C$-magnet is shown in Fig. 2.18. All dimensions are in cm. The relative permeability of the soft Fe yoke is 3000. If a current $I=1 \mathrm{amp}$ is to produce a field of about 100 gauss in the gap, how many turns of wire are required?

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A $C$-magnet is shown in Fig. 2.18. All dimensions are in cm. The relative permeability of the soft Fe yoke is 3000. If a current $I=1 \mathrm{amp}$ is to produce a field of about 100 gauss in the gap, how many turns of wire are required?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: No concrete image path/URL/filename is present; image fields contain only PIL image object representations.
metadata.reasoning_chain: Consider a cross section of the magnet parallel to the plane of the paper and denote its periphery, which is (including the gap) a square of sides $l=20 \mathrm{~cm}$, by $L$. As the normal component of $\mathbf{B}$ is continuous, the magnetic intensity in the gap is $B/\mu_{0}$, while that inside the magnet is $B/(\mu_{0}\mu_{r})$, where $\mu_{r}$ is the relative permeability of the iron. Ampere's circuital law $\oint_{L} \mathbf{H} \cdot d\ell = NI$ applied to $L$ becomes $\frac{B}{\mu_{0}}d+\frac{B}{\mu_{0}\mu_{r}}(4l-d)= NI$, where $d=2 \mathrm{~cm}$ is the width of the gap. Hence $N=\frac{B}{\mu_{0} I}\left[d+\frac{1}{\mu_{r}}(4l-d)\right] =\frac{100\times10^{-4}}{4\pi\times10^{-7}\times1}\left(0.02+\frac{0.2\times4-0.02}{3000}\right) =161 \text{ turns}$
